title: Intraday Source Item
description: Raw item fetched from any configured source before normalization. All source adapters (RSS, API, webhook, X/social) must produce this shape.

source_id.description: Unique identifier assigned by the originating source (e.g. RSS guid, API record id, tweet id).

source_slug.description: Stable registry identifier for the source (e.g. 'coindesk-rss', 'x-account-whale-alert'). Populated from the D1 sources table when available. Null for unconfigured or ad-hoc sources.

source_name.description: Human-readable label for the source (e.g. 'CoinDesk RSS', 'Reuters API').

source_type.description: Technical source category. x_account and x_query are first-class types for X (Twitter) monitoring. Use 'api' for official data releases, market data APIs, and structured feeds. Use 'rss' for news and research/official RSS/Atom feeds. Use 'social' for generic social channels (Telegram, Reddit).

source_url.description: Canonical URL of the original item. Null when not available.

title.description: Raw title or headline from the source.

body.description: Raw body text or description from the source. Null when not available.

author.description: Author name, journalist byline, or X account handle (@username) when provided by the source. Null when not available.

published_at.description: Publication timestamp from the source (ISO-8601). Null if the source does not provide one.

fetched_at.description: UTC timestamp when the item was fetched by the ingestion step (ISO-8601).

trust_tier.description: Trust classification from the source registry, passed through module boundaries when available. T1=Official, T2=Wire, T3=Specialist, T4=Signal/Social. Null for unconfigured sources.

trust_score.description: Numeric trust score (0–100) from the source registry, passed through module boundaries when available. Higher is more trusted. Null for unconfigured sources.

raw_json.description: Original source payload preserved for debugging and re-processing.

examples.title: Bitcoin Hits New All-Time High Above $120K

examples.author: Omkar Godbole

examples.body: We present empirical scaling laws for autoregressive language models trained at scales beyond 1 trillion parameters...
